**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Triggering the system-level end of the semiconductor rhapsody and the '4-person sensor chip design team (Fabless)' redrawing probe. The highly watered-down Apple transnational wafer foundry template in the original data has been physically completely written off. This enterprise, named 'Pierre Smart Sensing Technology,' has a business license that is a suffocating encyclopedia of the semiconductor industry: from 'integrated circuit manufacturing, chip manufacturing' to 'semiconductor discrete device manufacturing, optoelectronic device manufacturing.' The official system unhesitatingly hoisted it onto the altar of 'Electronic Device Manufacturing.' However, the forensic logic engine directly penetrated this extremely outrageous heavy-industry disguise, capturing its truest physical bottom line: the insured headcount is a mere 4 people! The paid-in capital is 555,000 RMB. The spatial coordinates are located at '2nd Floor, Unit B, No. 300 Zhihui Road, Qilin High-tech Industrial Development Zone.' The logical deduction instantly completed the demotion strike: in a 2nd-floor office of a high-tech zone office building, a boss leading 3 R&D personnel, carrying over 500,000 RMB in their pockets (not even enough to build a sink in a semiconductor factory), claims to have built a super wafer production line covering 'chips, integrated circuits, and semiconductor discrete devices'? The probe mercilessly deleted the word 'manufacturing' wherever it appeared in the license, and combining its company name 'Smart Sensing Technology,' locked onto what these 4 people sitting in front of computers can truly do: 'integrated circuit design; engineering and technological research; sales of electronic components and chips.' The truth is extremely clear: this is a 100% pure '4-Employee Fabless Sensor IC Design Studio' (an asset-light entity in a high-tech zone). They represent a typical Fabless (fabrication-less) model; the 4 people draw sensor chip blueprints and write underlying algorithms in the office, then find TSMC or SMIC for 'tape-out manufacturing,' and finally sell the packaged sensor chips (or IoT devices integrating the chips) to downstream customers. The official 'major electronics plant (wafer fab)' tag is the deepest common-sense misjudgment of the semiconductor industry chain, and has now been thoroughly demoted.
